name: United States
narrative: The primary architect of the tariff escalation cycle, seeking to rebalance bilateral deficits and restore domestic manufacturing capacity through the largest tariff increases since the Smoot-Hawley era.
narrativeZh: 此輪關稅升級的主要發動方，試圖透過近百年來最大規模的關稅加徵，縮減雙邊貿易逆差並重建國內製造業產能。

name: China
narrative: The primary target of U.S. tariff escalation, facing rates above 145% on most goods by 2025, triggering a structural retreat from its position as America's top import source since 2007.
narrativeZh: 美國關稅升級的主要目標，2025年多數商品面臨逾145%的關稅，導致中國自2007年以來首度失去美國最大進口來源國的地位，供應鏈加速外移。

name: Vietnam
narrative: The single largest beneficiary of U.S.-China trade diversion, absorbing electronics, furniture and apparel orders as manufacturers moved final assembly operations across the border from Guangdong province.
narrativeZh: 美中貿易轉移的最大單一受益國，隨著製造商將最終組裝從廣東跨境遷移，越南大量承接了電子、家具與成衣訂單，但也面臨「中國製造換殼」的審查壓力。

name: India
narrative: Capturing the smartphone assembly prize as Apple accelerated its India production strategy, while also gaining share in pharmaceuticals, textiles and chemicals as U.S. buyers diversified supply chains.
narrativeZh: 隨著蘋果加速印度生產布局，印度成功拿下智慧型手機組裝的關鍵份額，同時在製藥、紡織與化工領域也因美國買家分散供應鏈而獲得更多訂單。

name: Taiwan
narrative: Indispensable to the AI supply chain as the sole producer of leading-edge logic chips, Taiwan's export growth to the U.S. accelerated even as it navigated escalating strait tensions and Liberation Day tariff uncertainty.
narrativeZh: 作為全球唯一先進邏輯晶片量產地，台灣在AI供應鏈中的不可替代性使其對美出口持續成長，但台海地緣風險與「解放日」關稅的不確定性，也讓這份優勢愈來愈脆弱。

name: Thailand
narrative: Gaining share in hard drives, auto parts and electrical components as Japanese and Korean manufacturers used Thailand as a stable ASEAN production base with established logistics and a skilled industrial workforce.
narrativeZh: 泰國在硬碟、汽車零件與電氣元件領域穩定增加美國市佔，日韓廠商視其為東協內基礎設施完善、勞工技術成熟的穩定生產基地，持續擴大在當地的製造布局。

name: Malaysia
narrative: Emerging as a critical semiconductor test-and-packaging hub—particularly in Penang—as U.S. export controls on advanced technology pushed back-end chip work away from China and toward established Malaysian facilities.
narrativeZh: 隨著美國出口管制迫使晶片後段製程從中國轉移，馬來西亞檳城憑藉數十年的半導體產業積累，一躍成為全球最重要的晶片測試與封裝中心之一。

name: Mexico
narrative: Deeply embedded in U.S. supply chains through USMCA, Mexico absorbed near-shoring demand in auto, electronics and aerospace, but faces growing U.S. scrutiny over Chinese investment and transshipment through its territory.
narrativeZh: 透過《美墨加協定》深度嵌入美國供應鏈的墨西哥，承接了大量近岸外包需求，但美國對中資透過墨西哥規避關稅的審查日趨嚴格，使其「中間人」角色面臨政治風險。

name: European Union
narrative: Facing proposed 20% reciprocal tariffs before the April 9 pause, the EU has leveraged its collective market size to negotiate sector-specific carve-outs while preparing countermeasures on U.S. agriculture, steel and digital services.
narrativeZh: 歐盟面臨20%對等關稅提案，「解放日」暫停後雖獲喘息，但仍持續以整體市場規模為籌碼談判特定產業豁免，並備妥針對美國農產品、鋼鐵與數位服務的報復清單。

name: Japan
narrative: Caught between punishing U.S. auto tariffs and its critical role as a semiconductor equipment and materials supplier, Japan pursued a bilateral framework deal to protect its automotive industry while deepening technology and defense alignment with Washington.
narrativeZh: 夾在高額汽車關稅與半導體設備及材料關鍵供應商角色之間，日本積極推動雙邊框架協議以保護汽車產業，同時加深與華盛頓的科技與安保合作，試圖以戰略價值換取市場准入。

name: South Korea
narrative: A dual-role economy squeezed by reciprocal tariffs on its automotive exports while simultaneously benefiting from surging AI-driven demand for Samsung and SK Hynix high-bandwidth memory chips.
narrativeZh: 韓國扮演雙重角色：一方面汽車出口受對等關稅壓縮，另一方面三星與SK海力士的高頻寬記憶體卻因AI算力需求暴增而供不應求，使韓國在貿易戰中呈現苦樂不均的分裂局面。

name: Indonesia
narrative: Emerging as a textile and footwear supplier of last resort as buyers diversify beyond Vietnam's rising costs, while its position as the world's largest nickel producer gives it leverage in the EV battery materials transition.
narrativeZh: 隨著越南成本上升迫使買家進一步分散，印尼正成為成衣與鞋類的新興備選供應國；與此同時，作為全球最大鎳礦生產國，印尼在電動車電池材料供應鏈中握有獨特的戰略籌碼。
